channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Kaveri 2.0 Engine Revival: GTRE Plans Indigenous Power Boost for Tejas Mk1A Replacement in the 2030s
item.description: India’s long-struggling indigenous jet engine program is now entering a decisive new phase, with the Gas Turbine Research Establishment (GTRE) confirming plans for the development of the Kaveri 2.0 engine. This next-generation powerplant, derived from the Kaveri Dry Engine (KDE), is being positioned as a future replacement for the GE F404-IN20 engines currently powering the Tejas Mk1A fleet.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pinaka Takes Priority as Indian Army Reassesses 122mm Grad Rocket Replacement Plans
item.description: The Indian Army is preparing for a major transformation in its rocket artillery doctrine as it gradually phases out the ageing Russian-origin Grad systems and replaces them with additional units of the indigenous Pinaka multi-barrel rocket launcher (MBRL). Defence sources indicate that the Army’s long-term roadmap envisions expanding the Pinaka fleet to nearly 22 regiments, a move that could significantly reshape India’s battlefield rocket artillery capabilities.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: AMCA Set to Become World’s Cheapest 5th Generation Fighter Program in R&D Terms, Says ADA Official
item.description: India’s Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) program is on track to become one of the most cost-efficient fifth-generation fighter jet development programs in the world, according to a senior official from the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) speaking to idrw.org. The official revealed that the total research and development budget for AMCA stands at just $1.8 billion (around ?15,000 crore), making it one of the lowest-funded fifth-generation fighter programs globally.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO Prepares 100kW Laser Weapon Test for Indian Navy Destroyers Under DURGA-II Program
item.description: India is moving closer to deploying ship-mounted directed energy weapons as the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) prepares to test a 100-kilowatt high-power laser system designed for integration on frontline naval warships.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s Vision for a Sixth-Generation Fighter: A Network-Centric Flying Command Node Partnering with Europe
item.description: India’s pursuit of sixth-generation air combat capabilities marks a transformative shift from traditional fighter aircraft to highly integrated, intelligent systems designed for dominance in future multi-domain battlefields. Rather than focusing solely on a standalone jet, the envisioned platform embodies a network-centric, cloud-enabled architecture that redefines aerial warfare by prioritizing connectivity, data fusion, and collaborative operations over isolated performance.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Unmanned Dhruv and LUH Could Transform Military Logistics and Open New Civil Markets for HAL
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) stands at a strategic crossroads in India’s rapidly evolving defence and aerospace landscape. With the Advanced Light Helicopter (ALH) Dhruv having proven its reliability across military and civil roles over two decades, and the newer Light Utility Helicopter (LUH) demonstrating exceptional high-altitude performance, HAL has a golden opportunity to extend these platforms into unmanned cargo configurations. Transforming these helicopters into autonomous or remotely piloted cargo platforms could address pressing needs in both military logistics and civilian applications, while advancing India’s self-reliance goals under Atmanirbhar Bharat.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Air Dominance and Multi-Domain Integration – How the IAF Would Dominate Sindoor 2
item.description: Operation Sindoor 2 would elevate the Indian Air Force’s (IAF) role from supportive strikes to outright multi-domain dominance, building on its 2025 performance where it neutralized Pakistani counter-strikes and established air superiority within hours. With post-conflict budget boosts funding new Rafale squadrons and “Super Sukhoi” upgrades, the IAF would orchestrate a layered campaign fusing kinetic firepower, electronic warfare, cyber intrusions, and space-based assets—creating a “kill web” that blinds and paralyzes the enemy before the first bomb falls.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Conveys Deep Concern Over Hormuz Incident, Urges Iran To Facilitate India-Bound Ships Across Strait
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) on Saturday said that Iran’s Ambassador to India, Mohammad Fathali, was called in for a meeting with Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri over the shooting incident involving Indian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Iranian Ambassador Summoned After IRGC Gunboats Fire At Indian Vessels Transiting Hormuz
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) on Saturday summoned Iran’s Ambassador to India, Mohammad Fathali over the shooting incident involving Indian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. India is believed to have lodged a strong protest with Tehran over the incident as the Indian-flagged vessels – one of which is believed to be a Very Large Crude Carrier (VLCC) – came under fire from gunboats of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) even though the tanker and crew are reported to be safe.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Rajnath Singh-Led 4th Igom Reviews West Asia Developments; Calls Situation ‘Uncertain And Volatile’
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Saturday chaired the fourth meeting of the Informal Group of Ministers (IGoM), reviewing the situation in West Asia and assessing India’s preparedness amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Strongly Condemns Deadly Attack On French UN Peacekeepers In Lebanon
item.description: India on Saturday strongly condemned the attack on French UN Peacekeepers deployed at the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) in southern Lebanon, urging the need to investigate and bring perpetrators to justice.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Nationwide terror module busted; toy car IED plot foiled
item.description: Delhi Police has arrested four ‘radicalised’ men from Maharashtra, Odisha and Bihar who allegedly planned terror activities, carried out recce of sensitive locations to target them and attempted to recruit others through encrypted online platforms, officials said on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Army Personnel Injured During Parachute Accident in Ballari; Airlifted to Bengaluru
item.description: An army personnel sustained injuries on Friday morning after a parachute jump went wrong during an annual exercise in Ballari district. The accident occurred in the P.D. Halli region while a contingent of soldiers was undergoing routine parachute jump drills. Reports suggest that the parachute either failed to deploy or did not maneuver correctly during descent.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Nepal Govt’s Move To Control Purchases From Bordering Indian Towns Draws Flak
item.description: The Nepal government’s decision to levy customs duty on goods valued above NPR 100 brought from bordering Indian towns has drawn criticism from stakeholders in Nepal’s border areas, where nearby Indian markets have long served as a source of affordable goods.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Manipur: Two Naga Civilians Shot Dead In Ukhrul District; Ethnic Tensions Escalate
item.description: Two persons belonging to the Naga community were shot dead along the Imphal-Dimapur National Highway (NH-2) in Manipur on Saturday after unidentified armed assailants opened fire on vehicles heading towards Ukhrul district.?
item.dc:creator: admin
